meta.role: Discipline and enforcement layer — rules, gates, modes
meta.description: Transitional task list — will be replaced by MCP-based task-enforcer server when mature
meta.framework_reference: Eisenhower matrix (urgent/important), P1-P4 numerical scaling, RPE-weighted value scoring, PPAC decision gating, Kanban status flow

completed: MCP server with discipline tools (check, ci_check, get_mode, set_mode, lint_work, run_formatters, scan_secrets, standards_update, standards_verify, verify_commit)
Nine Standards data module with hash verification
Developer mode with baseline CI checks
Enhanced tasks.json v2.0 with priority, decision elements, testability, and Kanban status flow

pending.subject: Expand Nine Standards into automated MCP checks
pending.description: Each Standard should have a corresponding MCP tool or check function that can be run on demand or wired into CI. Standards: Consistent Defaults, Defense in Depth, Deterministic Ordering, No Dead Code, Single Source of Truth, Structural Honesty, etc.
references: .archive/ocd-old-reference/ (canonical historical source for Nine Standards, rules, gates, hooks, content pipelines, and enforcement configs: gitleaks.toml, semgrep.yml, trivy.yaml, hadolint.yaml, yamllint, mdformat.toml, etc.)
acceptance: Each standard has a callable checker
MCP exposes ocd_standard_check(name) or per-standard tools
Results posted in structured format (pass/fail + evidence)
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
RPE telemetry recorded on completion via ai rpe record --task ocd-1
Nine Standards check passes on all changed files
All ruff and mypy checks pass

pending.subject: Add MCP-pattern and PPAC-consistency validators
pending.description: Custom lint rules for MCP tool naming conventions (adhd_*, ocd_*, asd_*) and PPAC loop consistency checks (e.g., every Proposer must have a matching Critic update).
acceptance: Validator detects MCP tool naming violations
Validator warns on PPAC loop without outcome recording
Results usable in CI pipeline
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
Nine Standards check passes on changed files
Existing tests still pass at 100%

pending.subject: Implement additional modes (research, review, ops, personal)
pending.description: Each mode changes which standards are enforced and how strictly. Research mode might relax No Dead Code; Review mode might enforce stricter Structural Honesty; Ops mode emphasizes Defense in Depth; Personal mode is user-configurable.
acceptance: At least 2 new modes implemented with distinct rule sets
Mode switch via ocd_set_mode persists and affects checks
Documented in architecture
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
Mode switch is persistent across MCP reconnects
Documentation updated with mode descriptions

pending.subject: Create pre-commit hook integration
pending.description: Provide a script that agents can install as a git pre-commit hook. It runs ocd_check and blocks commits that violate the current mode's standards.
acceptance: Hook script runs ocd_check via MCP or direct call
Fails commit on critical violations
Optional per-repo install instructions
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
Hook tested against a known-violating commit
Install instructions documented in README

pending.subject: Document Nine Standards with examples
pending.description: Write clear documentation for each standard with concrete before/after code examples. This helps agents understand and apply the rules correctly.
acceptance: Each standard has a dedicated section with examples
Examples cover Python, Markdown, and JSON config
Linked from README
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
mdformat --check passes on all docs
Examples are runnable or verified correct

pending.subject: Expose OCD rules/skills via MCP for self-describing usage
pending.description: Follow the uniform `{package}_get_rules` pattern. Any agent can query `ocd_get_rules` to learn the Nine Standards, mode definitions, enforcement pipeline, and env var reference at runtime. **Cross-package pattern:** Coordinate with ADHD (adhd-7) and ASD (asd-6) to implement a unified pattern — implement once, replicate.
acceptance: New MCP tool ocd_get_rules returns structured protocol rules
Rules include: Nine Standards definitions, mode rule sets, hook pipeline, env var reference
Rules are versioned and match the current enforcement model
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
ocd_get_rules returns valid JSON matching ADHD's get_rules schema
Cross-repo pattern verified: ADHD, ASD, OCD all have get_rules
Human has approved the implementation via HITL

pending.subject: Align README MCP tool table and add Nine Standards links
pending.description: Update README.md to reflect all current MCP tools including ocd_get_rules. Add dedicated Nine Standards section with links to documentation. Ensure tool descriptions match the 'OCD Parallel' framing style.
acceptance: MCP tool table includes ocd_get_rules with parallel description
Nine Standards section added with dedicated subsections
All 10+ tools documented with consistent 'OCD Parallel' framing
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
README renders correctly on GitHub
No broken links or missing sections

pending.subject: Fix E501 line-too-long errors in mode_definitions.py
pending.description: 4 ruff E501 errors in src/ocd/modes/mode_definitions.py: long description strings for research, review, ops, and personal modes exceed the 100-char limit. Break lines or shorten descriptions.
acceptance: Zero E501 errors in mode_definitions.py
ruff check . passes with zero errors
Descriptions remain human-readable and clear
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
Zero ruff errors across entire repo
No regressions in existing tests

pending.subject: Bootstrap task-enforcer MCP server preparation
pending.description: Design and implement the transitional task-enforcer schema that will eventually become the full MCP-based task-enforcer server. This tasks.json v2.0 schema is the prototype. Create the data model, validation logic, and MCP tool stubs that can read/write tasks with full priority, decision, and RPE fields. Use the enhanced schema defined in this file as the contract.
acceptance: Schema matches tasks.json v2.0 structure exactly
Validation logic rejects malformed task entries
MCP tool stubs: ocd_task_list, ocd_task_get, ocd_task_update exist
RPE hook field is machine-readable and executable
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
Schema validates against all existing tasks
MCP tools are registered and respond to queries
Human has approved the enforcer design via HITL
RPE telemetry link established: task completion triggers RPE record

pending.subject: Integrate RPE telemetry for task completion signals
pending.description: Establish the RPE feedback loop between task completion and the Another-Intelligence dopamine/Critic system. Every task that reaches 'done' should generate a preference-pair training signal. Link to the existing ai rpe record command and ensure the ADHD bus carries completion events that the AI's Critic can consume.
acceptance: Task completion publishes RPE event to ADHD bus
Event includes: task_id, outcome value, context, timestamp
AI Critic can discover and consume RPE events from bus
Format compatible with preference-pair dataset generation
definition_of_done: All acceptance criteria verified
Test RPE event published and consumed end-to-end
Schema matches ai-4 preference-pair format
Nine Standards check passes on all new code
